pattern: Because the values are sorted, connectivity collapses to runs of consecutive elements whose neighbor gap never exceeds the threshold, so each node gets a component id in one pass.
hints: With the values sorted, the only edges that can matter for connectivity are between neighbors in sorted position.
A break occurs exactly where two consecutive values differ by more than the threshold, splitting the line into connected runs.
Sweep once assigning an incrementing component id, bumping it whenever the gap to the previous value exceeds maxDiff; a query is true iff both endpoints share an id.

edge_inputs.description: two nodes too far apart, plus self-loop query
maxDiff zero connects only equal adjacent values
gap exactly equal to maxDiff is connected

stress.description: sorted random values with random queries spanning components
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = sorted(rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n))
    maxDiff = rng.randint(0, 10)
    q = rng.randint(1, 200)
    queries = [[rng.randrange(n), rng.randrange(n)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [n, nums, maxDiff, queries]
stress.note: small maxDiff yields several components so both answers appear
